prose:
**Fukui Reactivity Analysis of Nitrobenzene** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, PySCF), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral state), with cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2) states. Geometry: user-supplied nitrobenzene.xyz (14 atoms). Fukui indices computed from Mulliken partial charges. All states converged. Charge conservation verified (sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000). 

**Most electrophilic site:** Carbon at position 4 (ortho to NO₂ group) with f⁺ = 0.496, indicating highest susceptibility to nucleophilic attack. **Most nucleophilic site:** Carbon at position 5 (meta to NO₂ group) with f⁻ = 0.107, indicating highest susceptibility to electrophilic attack. **Dual descriptor range:** −0.474 to +0.511, with largest magnitude at the ortho carbon (Δf = 0.511, electrophilic) and nitrogen (Δf = −0.474, nucleophilic). Results align with the known meta/para-directing effect of the electron-withdrawing NO₂ group. Charge scheme: Mulliken. No warnings reported. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy = true. Output: JSON at default path.
